name: Brooklyn Bridge Capital
description_short: Private hard money lender in NY-NJ offering bridge loans, construction financing, and commercial real estate debt solutions with 24-hour term sheets and 5-7 day closings.
description_long:
Brooklyn Bridge Capital is a private hard money and equity lender focused exclusively on the New York and New Jersey metropolitan area real estate market. The company positions itself as an alternative to traditional bank financing for commercial property owners, real estate investors, and brokers seeking faster access to capital. Their stated competitive advantage centers on speed of execution—offering term sheets within 24 hours and closing within 5-7 business days—substantially faster than conventional mortgage lenders.

The company offers a diversified portfolio of lending and debt/equity solutions tailored to commercial real estate scenarios. Their core services include bridge loans, construction financing, foreclosure bailouts, bank workouts, refinancing with cash-out options, property acquisitions, and debt acquisition (purchasing non-performing notes from financial institutions). They serve a broad range of commercial property types including multi-family residential, mixed-use, office, retail, warehouse, industrial, and specialized properties like self-storage and assisted living facilities. Recent loan examples range from $265,000 land loans to $30 million partnership buyouts.

What distinguishes Brooklyn Bridge Capital is their explicit focus on difficult-to-finance situations: foreclosed properties, non-owner-occupied residential properties, refinancing scenarios traditional banks reject, and distressed asset purchases. They target brokers, banks, and institutional clients alongside individual property owners, positioning themselves as a relief mechanism for banks offloading toxic or non-performing assets. Their equity-focused approach suggests willingness to work with borrowers who have limited conventional financing options.

A significant caveat is that hard money lending typically carries substantially higher interest rates and fees than conventional mortgages, and this company provides no rate, fee, or APR information on their website. The lack of transparency around actual costs makes it impossible to assess true affordability. Additionally, as a private lender serving a specific geographic region, their underwriting standards and risk tolerance are unknown, and there is no publicly verifiable information about licensing, regulatory compliance, or complaint history.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Documented speed: 24-hour term sheets and 5-7 business day closings vs. traditional 30-45 day mortgage timelines
Serves non-owner-occupied residential properties that most traditional lenders reject
Handles foreclosure bailouts and bank workout scenarios requiring specialized expertise
Large recent loan portfolio ($265K-$30M) demonstrates active lending capacity
Offers debt acquisition services, purchasing non-performing notes for institutional clients
Flexible property types accepted including mixed-use, industrial, self-storage, and assisted living
Equity-focused lending model may work with borrowers having limited conventional options
cons: No interest rates, fees, or APR disclosed—typical hard money loans carry 8-15%+ rates plus 2-5 point origination fees
Limited geographic service area (NY-NJ metro only) restricts availability for out-of-region borrowers
No information about regulatory licensing, complaint history, or third-party verification of claims
Private lender model means less transparency and consumer protections than regulated mortgage institutions
Website lacks borrower testimonials, independent reviews, or verifiable track record documentation
best_for: Real estate investors with commercial properties needing fast bridge or construction financing
Property owners facing foreclosure seeking rapid bailout capital
Commercial property buyers on tight acquisition timelines unable to use traditional mortgage financing
Real estate brokers and institutional lenders seeking to offload non-performing debt or toxic assets
diagnosis: Brooklyn Bridge Capital is best for experienced real estate investors and commercial property owners who need fast capital for time-sensitive transactions and can tolerate hard money lending terms (higher rates/fees) in exchange for speed and flexible underwriting. The critical caveat is that hard money is typically 2-4x more expensive than conventional mortgages—the website's complete lack of rate transparency makes it impossible to assess true affordability without direct contact.
services: Bridge loans for property acquisition gaps
Construction financing with interest-only payments during build phase
Commercial property refinancing with cash-out options
Non-performing note and defaulted loan purchase (debt acquisition)
Hard money lending for commercial real estate
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
